error.signature: RuntimeError: No RealSense devices were found
error.regex: (No RealSense devices|RealSense.*not found|Failed to set power state|rs2.*RS2_EXCEPTION|librealsense.*No device)

verdict.summary: Intel RealSense not detected. USB 2.0 port (D400 needs 3.0), missing udev rules, or kernel conflict.

dead_ends.why_fails: pip pyrealsense2 is just the wrapper; you need udev rules and kernel patches from official repo

dead_ends.action: Use USB 2.0 port for D400 series
dead_ends.why_fails: D435/D455 require USB 3.0 for depth streaming. May enumerate on 2.0 but depth fails.

dead_ends.action: Compile librealsense from source on Ubuntu LTS
dead_ends.why_fails: Official APT repo with DKMS patches is easier and more reliable

workarounds.action: Install from official APT repository with kernel patches

workarounds.how: lsusb -t | grep realsense  # speed should show 5000M not 480M

workarounds.action: Install udev rules for non-root access
workarounds.how: sudo cp config/99-realsense-libusb.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/ && sudo udevadm control --reload-rules
